creator: Tim Fernholz
title: (No AI) Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident

content: Anthropic executives said it was an accident and retracted the bulk of the takedown notices.

title: (No AI) AI can push your Stream Deck buttons for you
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/24595940/236572_BOTM_Stream_Deck_DSeifert_0008.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

If you're tired of controlling Stream Deck devices by manually pushing buttons, then good news: Elgato will now let you delegate that task to a chatbot instead. The [Stream Deck 7.4 software update](https://www.elgato.com/ww/en/explorer/products/stream-deck/sd-mcp-setup/) released today introduces Model Context Protocol (MCP) support, allowing AI assistants like Claude, ChatGPT, and Nvidia G-Assist to find and activate Stream Deck actions on your behalf.

"You still set up actions in Stream Deck app the same way you always have. MCP adds a new way to trigger them," Elgato said in its announcement. "Once everything is connected, you can type or speak requests and your AI tool will trigger the matching Stream Deck act …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/905021/elgato-stream-deck-mcp-ai-agent-update)

"You still set up actions in Stream Deck app the same way you always have. MCP adds a new way to trigger them," Elgato said in its announcement. "Once everything is connected, you can type or speak requests and your AI tool will trigger the matching Stream Deck act …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Baidu’s robotaxis froze in traffic, creating chaos
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/gettyimages-2152484525.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

A Baidu Apollo robotaxi in Wuhan, China. | Image: Bloomberg via Getty Images

Numerous robotaxis operated by Chinese tech giant Baidu froze in a major city on Tuesday, [reportedly](https://www.wired.com/story/robotaxi-outage-in-china-leaves-passengers-stuck-in-cars-on-highways/) trapping passengers inside, stranding them on highways, and causing at least one accident in snarled traffic.

Police in Wuhan [confirmed](https://www.wsj.com/business/autos/baidus-apollo-go-robotaxis-stall-in-chinas-wuhan-a9b143ad?gaa_at=eafs&gaa_n=AWEtsqdXqKfhF249d_igjLuqLB_jaDXOeS-bCd2J2YpiqfM32h2GexPQC-gvHS-S1T0%3D&gaa_ts=69cce8c4&gaa_sig=zzFxk7kh7UvuBHX6zaCAuT1WfVKyb9b5r3tHHTtsXl_SGmComAeS5mCdWQrqstZCDQFPhWznjBJxrto19n1C5A%3D%3D) receiving multiple reports of Baidu's Apollo Go robotaxis stopping in the middle of streets and being unable to move. Police said no injuries have been reported and that preliminary investigations suggest an unspecified "system failure" is responsible for the outage.

> NEW: Dozens of robotaxis by Baidu stopped on the road in Wuhan, causing crashes on highways and trapping passengers in the cars-some for mo …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/905012/baidu-apollo-robotaxi-freeze-china)
contentSnippet:
A Baidu Apollo robotaxi in Wuhan, China. | Image: Bloomberg via Getty Images	

Numerous robotaxis operated by Chinese tech giant Baidu froze in a major city on Tuesday, reportedly trapping passengers inside, stranding them on highways, and causing at least one accident in snarled traffic. 
Police in Wuhan confirmed receiving multiple reports of Baidu's Apollo Go robotaxis stopping in the middle of streets and being unable to move. Police said no injuries have been reported and that preliminary investigations suggest an unspecified "system failure" is responsible for the outage. 


NEW: Dozens of robotaxis by Baidu stopped on the road in Wuhan, causing crashes on highways and trapping passengers in the cars-some for mo …


Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Numerous robotaxis operated by Chinese tech giant Baidu froze in a major city on Tuesday, reportedly trapping passengers inside, stranding them on highways, and causing at least one accident in snarled traffic. Police in Wuhan confirmed receiving multiple reports of Baidu's Apollo Go robotaxis stopping in the middle of streets and being unable to move. […]

creator: Will Knight
title: (No AI) AI Models Lie, Cheat, and Steal to Protect Other Models From Being Deleted
dc:creator: Will Knight
content: A new study from researchers at UC Berkeley and UC Santa Cruz suggests models will disobey human commands to protect their own kind.
contentSnippet: A new study from researchers at UC Berkeley and UC Santa Cruz suggests models will disobey human commands to protect their own kind.

creator: John Semley
title: (No AI) ‘Thank You for Generating With Us!’ Hollywood's AI Acolytes Stay on the Hype Train
dc:creator: John Semley
content: Star Wars producer Kathleen Kennedy was one of the few skeptics at the Runway AI Summit, where AI was compared to fire and the printing press just a week after Sora’s death.
contentSnippet: Star Wars producer Kathleen Kennedy was one of the few skeptics at the Runway AI Summit, where AI was compared to fire and the printing press just a week after Sora’s death.
